pr.title: Remove broken "Upload progress" portion of build job
pr.author: PsiLupan

summary: Removed the broken GitHub Actions "Upload progress" step from the build workflow. The deleted step only ran for link-mode builds on the default branch, used the PROGRESS_API_KEY secret, and invoked tools/upload_progress.py to send build progress.json data to progress.decomp.club. The PR left the following "Upload map" artifact step intact.
what_changed: Removed the conditional `if: matrix.mode == 'link' && github.ref_name == github.event.repository.default_branch` associated with progress upload.
Removed use of the `PROGRESS_API_KEY` secret from this workflow step.
Removed invocation of `python tools/upload_progress.py` with base URL `https://progress.decomp.club/`, project `melee`, matrix version, and `build/${{matrix.version}}/progress.json`.
Kept the subsequent `Upload map` artifact step, which still runs for `matrix.mode == 'link'`.
smart_moves: Removed a known-broken CI publishing step instead of allowing it to continue as a tolerated failure.
Eliminated dependency on an external progress service and secret from the build path, reducing CI fragility.
Kept map artifact upload behavior separate from progress publishing, avoiding broader workflow disruption.
decomp_lessons: CI steps that publish decomp progress to external services should be isolated from core build/link validation so broken reporting does not obscure actual build status.
If progress reporting infrastructure becomes stale or unavailable, removing the upload step can be preferable to keeping dead `continue-on-error` automation.
Workflow maintenance matters for decomp projects because reliable build artifacts and CI signal are more valuable than broken ancillary reporting.
naming_conventions: The removed workflow step was named `Upload progress`.
The workflow used uppercase secret/env naming with `PROGRESS_API_KEY`.
The removed upload command used project identifier `melee` for the progress service.
The build matrix version was referenced as `${{matrix.version}}` and progress file path as `build/${{matrix.version}}/progress.json`.
review_feedback: No issue comments, review comments, or review records were present in the provided PR slice.
follow_up_queries: Check whether `tools/upload_progress.py` remains in the repository and whether it is still used by any other workflow or script.
Search for other references to `PROGRESS_API_KEY` to determine whether the secret can be retired.
Search later PRs for restoration or replacement of progress upload infrastructure.
